question: Compare the operating pressure listed in Table 1.2-1 with the design pressure in Table 5.3-1 for the RPV.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It relies on passive safety systems, including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems, and is housed in a steel containment partially immersed in water for effective passive heat removal. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering through control rods and soluble boron adjustments, with all major components integrated to minimize external piping and enhance safety.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the Combined License (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0706.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.B Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0706.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 regarding events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, closure of the main steam isolation valve, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), specifically for fuel cladding integrity. It notes that NuScale does not have a steam pressure regulator and that the design conforms to requirements for incidents of moderate frequency, instrument setpoints, single failures, and performance of nonsafety-related systems, with no exceptions or additional comments.

nuscale_ch01_0709.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.E Most Limiting Plant System Single Failure
nuscale_ch01_0709.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets regulatory criteria for various postulated events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), even though the design does not include a steam pressure regulator. For each scenario, the analysis concludes that fuel cladding integrity is maintained, incidents of moderate frequency are addressed, instrument setpoints are appropriate, and the most limiting single failure is considered, with no exceptions or non-conformances identified.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, clarifies the use of representative conceptual design information (CDI), and notes that each COL applicant must reference the NuScale FSAR while supplying site-specific details as required by regulations.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies compliance with ASME BPVC and NQA-1 standards for handling and storage, as well as environmental controls during shipment, and lists the key reference documents used.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all predicted values remain within regulatory safety criteria.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component and barrier to fission product release, supporting various reactor systems and components, and is constructed and certified according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity. The materials used for the RPV are carefully selected to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary throughout the plant's design life.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with overpressure protection requirements by referencing applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) code cases, as mandated by various sections of 10 CFR 50.55a and related Regulatory Guides (RGs). It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet NRC-endorsed standards, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance across its design power range. It outlines the criteria and parameters used to optimize plant operation, such as coolant temperatures and flow rates, and explains that calculations are performed using thermal hydraulic software and verified manually. The analysis accounts for uncertainties and confirms that the reactor’s natural circulation design results in low pressure losses, supporting efficient and reliable operation.

nuscale_ch01_0722.title: DSRS 15.2.7, Rev 0: Loss of Normal Feedwater FlowII.1 Fuel and System Pressure Parameters met
nuscale_ch01_0722.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for various design basis events, including loss of normal feedwater flow. It confirms that fuel and system pressure parameters are met, events are appropriately categorized, and analytical models conform to requirements, with no exceptions noted for DSRS 15.2.7.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and is engineered to prevent fuel design limit violations during all operational conditions.

edges.description: Section B references Section A by stating that Section 1.2 (from Section A) identifies the structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, thus connecting the operating characteristics in Section A to the interface requirements in Section B.

Section A provides the operating characteristics relevant to the RPV, while Section B addresses regulatory conformance for accident scenarios affecting RPV pressure, connecting them through the need to compare actual operating pressures to design and regulatory limits.

Section A provides the operating pressure values for the RPV, while Section B specifies the design pressure limits that must not be exceeded, allowing a direct comparison to determine compliance.
